nodes.name: run.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses sub-commands (think, fix, chat, bot, web, resume) and launches the appropriate pipeline mode.

nodes.name: bot.py
nodes.summary: Telegram bot interface that lets users send tasks from a phone and receive approval-request messages as replies.

nodes.name: chat.py
nodes.summary: Persistent CLI chat mode that maintains conversation history on disk and supports /build, /fix, /status commands.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Main LangGraph orchestrator that wires all 20+ agent nodes into the pipeline DAG, handles routing, fan-out verification, and session checkpointing.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph TypedDict state schema defining all shared fields (phase, specs, findings, messages) with custom reducers for message capping and work-item replacement.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Multi-provider LLM configuration supporting 12 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.) with auto-detection from model name and per-agent model overrides.

nodes.name: bus.py
nodes.summary: Thread-safe agent-to-agent pub/sub message bus backed by SQLite, supporting publish, consume with read cursors, thread tracking, and in-memory reset.

nodes.name: observability.py
nodes.summary: Langfuse observability integration for logging LLM calls, token usage, and agent traces.

nodes.name: otel.py
nodes.summary: OpenTelemetry setup for distributed tracing across agent pipeline runs.

nodes.name: react_loop.py
nodes.summary: Shared ReAct tool-use loop with exponential-backoff retry, token tracking, cost estimation, OpenRouter key rotation, and progress callbacks used by all coding agents.

nodes.name: coder.py
nodes.summary: Senior Developer agent that writes production code using the ReAct loop, supports fix-iteration mode focusing only on reported issues, and reports all changed files.

nodes.name: planner.py
nodes.summary: Planner agent that decomposes architecture specs into prioritised WorkItem tasks for the coder to execute incrementally.

nodes.name: architect.py
nodes.summary: Solution Architect agent that reads existing code via file tools and produces a system design spec with human approval gate.

nodes.name: reviewer.py
nodes.summary: Tech Lead reviewer agent that performs code review and emits structured AgentFinding objects with severity levels.

nodes.name: tester.py
nodes.summary: QA Engineer agent that writes and runs tests, parses test output, and categorizes failure types for targeted coder fixes.

nodes.name: security.py
nodes.summary: Security auditor agent that performs OWASP-aligned static analysis and emits critical/warn findings for the coder to fix.

nodes.name: debugger.py
nodes.summary: Debugger agent that inspects runtime errors, traces exceptions, and produces a structured debug report.

nodes.name: auditor.py
nodes.summary: Code auditor agent that checks for code quality, complexity, and architectural consistency issues.

nodes.name: evaluator.py
nodes.summary: Engineering Manager agent that aggregates all verification findings and decides whether to ship (goto learn_lessons) or retry (goto coder) up to max_iterations.

nodes.name: requirements.py
nodes.summary: Product Manager agent that writes a PRD from user task description with human approval gate at the requirements phase.

nodes.name: designer.py
nodes.summary: UI/UX agent that generates component code and design specifications from requirements with human approval gate.

nodes.name: docs.py
nodes.summary: Documentation agent that generates or updates README and inline docs after code passes evaluation.

nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: Persistent lesson storage that saves and loads learned patterns per project to ~/.ai-dev-team/memory/ for cross-session memory.

nodes.name: project_detector.py
nodes.summary: Auto-detects project language, framework, code style, and frontend target by reading CLAUDE.md, pyproject.toml, package.json and inspecting the file tree.

nodes.name: codebase_indexer.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight codebase indexer that scans source files to build a symbol map of classes, functions, API endpoints, and DB models.

nodes.name: import_healer.py
nodes.summary: Post-coding node that detects and fixes broken import statements left by the coder agents before committing.

nodes.name: frontend_web.py
nodes.summary: Web frontend coder specialised for React/Vue/HTML+CSS projects with web-specific conventions.

nodes.name: frontend_mobile.py
nodes.summary: Mobile frontend coder specialised for React Native / Flutter projects.

nodes.name: frontend_desktop.py
nodes.summary: Desktop frontend coder specialised for Electron / Tauri / PyQt projects.

nodes.name: shell_tools.py
nodes.summary: Sandboxed file and shell tools with command allowlist (no rm/sudo/kill), path restriction to project directory, and credential scrubbing.

nodes.name: rag_tools.py
nodes.summary: LangChain tool wrapping the RAG store search, exposes search_codebase to agents for semantic code lookup.

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: RAG vector store that builds diff-aware embeddings per project, supports incremental updates, and provides hybrid semantic+BM25 search with RRF fusion.

nodes.name: chunker.py
nodes.summary: Splits source files into overlapping text chunks suitable for embedding, respecting file type boundaries.

nodes.name: hybrid_search.py
nodes.summary: Combines cosine-similarity semantic search with BM25 keyword ranking using Reciprocal Rank Fusion for better code search results.

nodes.name: lessons_rag.py
nodes.summary: Indexes and retrieves previously learned lessons via semantic search so only relevant past lessons are injected into the current pipeline context.

nodes.name: app.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI live dashboard with WebSocket streams for team messages and live output, REST endpoints for pause/resume/inject/abort pipeline controls, and optional Redis state sharing.

nodes.name: a2a_routes.py
nodes.summary: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol routes exposing agent cards and task endpoints for inter-agent HTTP communication.

nodes.name: agui_routes.py
nodes.summary: AG-UI protocol routes for streaming agent events over Server-Sent Events or WebSocket to frontend UIs.

nodes.name: a2a_cards.py
nodes.summary: Defines A2A agent card data structures that describe each agent's capabilities, inputs, and outputs.

nodes.name: build_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs and compiles the full LangGraph StateGraph with all 20+ nodes, conditional edges, fan-out verification, and SQLite or in-memory checkpointer.

nodes.name: init_node
nodes.summary: Pipeline initialisation node: detects project context, builds codebase index, initialises RAG, loads relevant lessons, and creates a git branch for the task.

nodes.name: fan_out_verification
nodes.summary: LangGraph Send fan-out that spawns reviewer, tester, security, debugger, and auditor agents in parallel after coding.

nodes.name: react_loop
nodes.summary: Core ReAct execution loop: iterates LLM inference and tool calls up to max_iterations, tracking changed files and token usage throughout.

nodes.name: get_llm
nodes.summary: Factory that dynamically imports the correct LangChain LLM class for the configured provider and returns a ready-to-use instance.

nodes.name: AgentBus
nodes.summary: Thread-safe pub/sub bus with SQLite persistence: supports publish, consume-with-cursor, thread loading for session restore, and reset for pipeline start.

nodes.name: State
nodes.summary: LangGraph TypedDict defining all shared pipeline state: task, phase, specs, findings, work items, control flags, token counts, and conversation messages.

nodes.name: TokenUsage
nodes.summary: Dataclass tracking cumulative input/output token counts and estimated cost across all LLM calls in a session.

nodes.name: ControlState
nodes.summary: Dashboard control state (paused, inject_message, skip, abort) with Redis-backed properties that fall back transparently to in-memory storage.

tours.title: Getting Started — How a Task Becomes Code

steps.reason: Start here: the CLI entry point that accepts a task description and kicks off the pipeline.
The main orchestrator builds the LangGraph DAG and wires all agents together.
The first node executed: detects the project, indexes the codebase, builds RAG, and creates a git branch.
Product Manager agent gathers requirements from the task description with a human approval gate.
Architect reads existing code and produces a technical design, which the user approves.
Coder implements the architecture spec using the ReAct loop with file and shell tools.
Evaluator aggregates all verification findings and decides to ship or send back to coder.

tours.title: Core Infrastructure — State, Bus, RAG, and Tools

steps.reason: All data flows through this TypedDict — understand it to understand the pipeline.
Agents communicate via this pub/sub bus backed by SQLite for persistence across sessions.
Every coding agent shares this ReAct loop with retry, token tracking, and progress reporting.
The sandboxed tool set that agents use: file read/write, shell commands restricted by allowlist.
Vector store providing semantic code search with diff-aware incremental updates.
The live dashboard exposes WebSocket streams and REST controls for monitoring and intervention.
